creator: gneff@trioptimum.com
text:
I obtained some help in building a debug version, running it in gdb, and
setting a break point.

When I do what triggers the crash, gdb spit out:

Program received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
[Switching to Thread 0x8103000 (LWP 100151)]
0x293dbc5c in PR_Lock () from /usr/local/lib/libplds4.so.1

That library is part of the devel/nspr port of which I have nspr-4.6.7.

-G

/*
 * Glen R. J. Neff

 * safety deserve neither liberty nor safety."
 *                 -- Benjamin Franklin, 1759
